questions.text: In an adiabatic process, the state of a gas is changed from P1 , V1 , T1 to P2 , V2 , T2 . Which of the following relation is correct?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: In an adiabatic process we know that PV= constant. So, P1V1= P2V2 and from the ideal gas equation we know that P= nRTV and by substituting it in the above equation we get nRT1V1V1= nRT2V2V2 where n= number of moles; R= universal Gas constant; T1= temperature 1 of gas in Kelvin; T2= temperature 2 of gas in Kelvin; V1= volume 1 of gas; V2= volume 2 of gas. As n and R are both constants they can be cancelled out leaving us with T1V1V1= T2V2V2 . Option A: T1V1V1= T2V2V2 can also be written as T1 V1-1V1= T2 V2-1V2 V1and V2 can be taken common on both sides and the equation can be written as T1X V1-1= T2XV2-1 so option A is correct
Option B: By looking at the original relationship P1V1= P2V2 we can mark option B as incorrect because the power of V is and not -1.
Option C: The relationship is P1V1= P2V2 and there is no temperature unlike that shown in option C T1P1 = T2P2. Also the P is not raised to the power volume is so option C is incorrect
The relationship is P1V1= P2V2 and there is no temperature unlike that shown in option D T1V1 = T2V2 so option D is incorrect

questions.explanation: In an adiabatic process we know that PV= constant. So, P1V1= P2V2 and from the ideal gas equation we know that P= nRTV and by substituting it in the above equation we get nRT1V1V1= nRT2V2V2 where n= number of moles; R= universal Gas constant; T1= temperature 1 of gas in Kelvin; T2= temperature 2 of gas in Kelvin; V1= volume 1 of gas; V2= volume 2 of gas. As n and R are both constants they can be cancelled out leaving us with T1V1V1= T2V2V2 . Option A: T1V1V1= T2V2V2 can also be written as T1 V1-1V1= T2 V2-1V2 V1and V2 can be taken common on both sides and the equation can be written as T1X V1-1= T2XV2-1 so option A is correct

options.explanation: As per the explanation, this option is incorrect.

As vrms of hydrogen =Vh Vrms of nitrogen =Vn Vrms of oxygen = Vo Vrms is given as = √3RT/M Where R is constant, T is temperature. So Mh =1 Mn = 14 Mo = 16. So Mo > Mn >Mh. Then VH > VN > VO As its proof from the equation, so no need to check the other options. Hence option b is correct
As per the explanation, this option is incorrect.
As per the explanation, this option is incorrect.

Where M denotes the molar mass of the element.So Mo > Mn >Mh. Then VH > VN > VO because velocity has inverse relation with mass.
